body.title: [triage] 67 files SELF-APPOINT as canonical. Canonicity must trace to an INTENTION — but only 3 intentions exist, so WRITE THE INTENTIONS FIRST.
body.description:
**⛔ FAIL — KICKED BACK TO DESIGN 2026-07-13 (Akien: 'those should get sent back to the design step as FAIL for learning from and fixing').**

**THIS TICKET ENTERED SPRINT WITH NO INTENTION.** Under D-intentions-are-the-root-2026-07-13 and proof-on-close, THE INTENTION IS THE PROPERTY MUTATION-RED BREAKS. With no intention there is **no property to break**, so this ticket **cannot be proven as it stands** — it could only ever close hollow, or `shipped-unproven` for a reason that is not the real one.

**IT IS NOT DEFECTIVE WORK — IT IS INCOMPLETE DESIGN.** So the honest disposition is BACK TO DESIGN, not a backfilled intention. CC drafting the missing intention would have **papered over the real defect**: 150 tickets cleared the design step without an intention, which is a **gate hole**, not 150 individual oversights. Per CLAUDE.md, the nuisance stays VISIBLE so it drives finding the fix — never papered over, which sucks silently forever (CP4).

**CP2 — FAIL = FURTHER ADVANCE IN LEARNING.** This kickback IS the learning signal. The fix is upstream: the triage→sprint transition must reject a missing, empty, or degenerate (`TBD`/`unknown`) intention (T-sprint-tickets-with-no-intention-cannot-be-proven).

**TO RE-ENTER SPRINT:** state the intention as a present-tense contract — *'I intend that X, because Y'* — where X names a property a hollow build would violate. Akien declares it (`provenance_class: declared`); a machine-extracted one is a RATCHET, not a SPEC (D-provenance-is-the-safety-property-2026-07-13).

---

Akien 2026-07-13, correcting CC.0's first draft: 'it's not age. ... it's before we started thinking in terms of intention. because the intentions are the North Star. even though they can conflict with one another, as a body, once their conflicts are resolved, that's where we're going always.'

THE RULE (R-canonicity-is-granted-not-asserted): a file may not declare itself canonical. Canonicity is a GRANT that traces back to an INTENTION: intention -> decision -> file. A claim with no path back to an intention has NO AUTHORITY, regardless of age. AGE IS NOT THE TRIGGER — CC.0 got that wrong; a file written yesterday can assert a canonicity that contradicts the intentions.

MEASURED: 67 files assert canonicity ('canonical source' / 'single source' / 'source of truth' / 'authoritative' / 'master copy'). The store holds THREE intentions.

** THEREFORE THE ORDER OF WORK INVERTS. ** You cannot check 67 claims against 3 stars. The North Star is barely mapped. Building the sweep first would produce a GREEN CHECK THAT MEANS NOTHING — the exact passing-check-that-checks-nothing failure this rule exists to kill (and which bit us 4x on 2026-07-12: questions.json's false canonicity; the 51% stale headline; home_db_url vs make_home_proxy; 8 orphan skills invisible to every audit).

SO:
  STEP 1 (the real work): WRITE THE INTENTIONS DOWN. The body of intentions must be rich enough to check against. This is not a tooling task — it is Akien's North Star, mostly still in his head. Intentions MAY conflict; resolving those conflicts IS the work, not a defect.
  STEP 2: for each canonicity claim, trace it to an intention. It either EARNS a grant or LOSES the claim (strip the words).
  STEP 3: fold the check into validity_sweep.py (day-close; no new tool — homogeneity over special-case). Print `canonicity: asserted=N granted=M ungranted=K`. Annotate, never delete (CP2).

**Test plan:** red->green: validity_sweep reports the ungranted-canonicity count; a fixture asserting canonicity with no path to an intention is FLAGGED; one with a grant is not. NOTE: this test is only MEANINGFUL after step 1 — a green run against 3 intentions proves nothing.

**Rule:** rule:R-canonicity-is-granted-not-asserted
body.fail_reason: entered sprint with no intention — cannot be proven; design incomplete
